pi_items.source: Analyzed legacy_sales_dump.csv from jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 create.sql. Counted unique CustomerName values in the flattened CSV (Orders × Customers × OrderDetails × Products × Categories join). Found exactly 89 unique customer names, which represents customers with at least one order. The full Customers table has 93 rows, but 4 customers have no orders and are therefore not in the CSV.
Analyzed legacy_sales_dump.csv from jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 create.sql. Counted unique ProductName values. Found exactly 77 unique products.
Analyzed legacy_sales_dump.csv from jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 create.sql. Counted unique LegacyOrderID values. Found exactly 830 unique orders.

Analyzed legacy_sales_dump.csv from jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 create.sql. Counted data rows in the generated CSV (excluding header). Found exactly 2155 data rows, which equals the number of OrderDetail records in the source database.

Examined setup_task.sh script. The script attempts to download from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3/main/Northwind_large.sql, which does not exist and causes the script to fail. However, the actual data source that matches the metadata counts is the create.sql file from the jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 repository. Downloaded and verified both files.

pi_items.verified_value: Real data from public Microsoft Northwind sample database converted to SQLite3. The database represents a small business (Northwind Traders) with customers, products, categories, suppliers, orders, and order details from the 1990s.
pi_items.source: Cross-referenced https://github.com/jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 README and created database from SQL source. The jpwhite3 repository is a well-known conversion of the Microsoft Northwind sample database to SQLite3.

pi_items.verified_value: CRITICAL ISSUE: setup_task.sh will fail. The script tries to download from URL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3/main/Northwind_large.sql which returns 404 Not Found. This file does not exist in the repository.
pi_items.source: Tested direct download of the URL specified in setup_task.sh line 25. Received HTTP 404 error. Searched GitHub API for the file - it does not exist. The correct source files are: (1) dist/northwind.db (pre-built database, ~24MB, contains expanded data with 830 orders but 609K+ order details), or (2) src/create.sql (SQL source with standard Northwind data matching metadata).

pi_items.verified_value: OrderItems row count equals CSV data rows (minus header). Verified: 2155 OrderItems = 2155 CSV data rows (2156 total with header).
pi_items.source: Generated legacy_sales_dump.csv from the create.sql Northwind database using the exact query from setup_task.sh lines 69-86. Result: 2156 lines total (1 header + 2155 data), corresponding to the 2155 rows in Order Details table.

privileged_info_summary: The Northwind database used for this task is real data from the public Microsoft Northwind sample database, converted to SQLite3 by the jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3 GitHub project. The normalized table counts are verified: Customers=89 (unique customers with orders), Products=77, Orders=830, OrderItems=2155. These values come from the standard Northwind dataset (create.sql), not the expanded version. Each OrderItem row corresponds to one row in the legacy_sales_dump.csv file, which is a flattened join of Orders, Customers, OrderDetails, Products, and Categories tables. The setup script has a critical bug: it attempts to download Northwind_large.sql from GitHub, which does not exist (404 Not Found). The actual data source should be https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3/main/src/create.sql (for metadata-matching counts) or https://github.com/jpwhite3/northwind-SQLite3/raw/main/dist/northwind.db (for a pre-built database with expanded data).
